title: Mean amino acid biosynthetic cost anti-correlates with human protein abundance after controlling for GC, length, disorder, conservation, secretion, and gene age

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'Mean amino acid biosynthetic cost anti-correlates with human protein abundance a'
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

source.note: Protein-coding genes from GENCODE v44 hg38; one canonical isoform per gene.
Per-gene protein abundance (ppm) from PaxDb v6.0 integrated whole-organism human dataset.
Per-residue ATP biosynthetic cost from Akashi & Gojobori 2002. 20-row lookup table.

Per-gene covariates: gc_content (over CDS), cds_length, mean phyloP (conservation), IUPred2A protein disorder fraction, UniProt secreted flag, gene_age (Ensembl gene tree). Assembled in mod02_extended_confounders.R.

operations.note: Per-gene mean cost = mean(cost over residues in canonical isoform). Logically: join genes -> residues -> cost table -> aggregate. Modeled here as a single per-gene mean-cost computation.

statistics.name: spearman_rho_bivariate
statistics.note: Bivariate Spearman, no controls. Direction matches Akashi-Gojobori prediction; magnitude is biologically modest (~1.3% per-quintile cost gradient) but statistically overwhelming at human transcriptome scale.

statistics.name: spearman_p_two_sided
statistics.note: Reported as 'overwhelming' in MOD-3; pinned here as upper bound 1e-100 for inference threshold purposes.

statistics.name: spearman_partial_rho_six_controls
statistics.note: Partial Spearman after simultaneous control for GC, length, disorder, conservation, secreted, gene_age. Pinned to MOD-2 acceptance threshold (|partial r| > 0.03). Actual value to be filled in by evaluate.py from results/mod02_confounder_table.csv when run.

inference.justification: Three conjuncts: (1) |partial r| > 0.03 after simultaneous control of all six confounders is the MOD-2 pre-registered survival threshold; (2) the partial r must remain negative (cost anti-correlates with abundance), encoding the directional Akashi-Gojobori prediction rather than a generic 'something correlates'; (3) bivariate p < 0.01 is a sanity backstop. Note: the inference licenses ONLY the consistent-with-symbolic-channel-selection conclusion. The dual-channel SELECTION mechanism is encoded as a separate hypothesized claim with a 'supersedes' edge to the original draft's overclaiming language (per MOD-1).
inference.failure_mode: If s_partial_rho_full_controls falls within [-0.03, 0.03], the cost-expression signal collapses to a confounder. If sign flips positive, Akashi-Gojobori is falsified for human. Either failure dissolves the dual-channel-consistent reading.

conclusion.assertion: Across human protein-coding genes, mean amino acid biosynthetic cost anti-correlates with PaxDb protein abundance at bivariate Spearman rho = -0.145; the partial correlation survives simultaneous control for GC, length, disorder, conservation, secretion, and gene age. This is consistent with symbolic-channel (codon identity) selection on translation cost; it does not by itself demonstrate dual-channel selection.

scope.scale_note: Per-gene aggregation. The transcript-level analysis (per-isoform cost) and the codon-level analysis (per-codon usage frequency) live as sibling claim objects.

external_assumptions.statement: PaxDb integrated whole-organism abundance is a defensible proxy for in-vivo per-gene protein abundance averaged across human tissues.
external_assumptions.citation: Wang et al. PaxDb 2015; PaxDb v6.0 2023

external_assumptions.statement: Akashi-Gojobori biosynthetic cost (ATP per residue) is a defensible proxy for fitness-relevant translation cost in human; cross-species transfer of bacterial costs to a human Ne is non-trivial but the rank ordering is preserved.

Six controls (GC, length, disorder, phyloP, secreted, gene_age) are sufficient to bound the residual confounder space; gene-family duplication count and sub-cellular organelle membership are uncontrolled and could absorb additional signal.

The MOD-1 reframe from 'demonstrates dual selection' to 'is consistent with' is the correct epistemic register; the bivariate correlations licensed by this claim do not establish opposing selective regimes on the same mutations.
